what_it_does: Acceptance test for the F3 chunk-and-compose over-context tier in runtime/cognition.py. It verifies that run_chunked reads the model context window from the live registry, splits oversized input into deterministic chunks, maps a role over each chunk via run_items, composes the per-chunk outputs into one result via run_reduce, remains byte-identical to a single run_role call when input fits, and fails loudly if any chunk fails.
observations: Probes a live resident 4B model and falls back to a mocked transport if it is unavailable.
Asserts the registry context window equals ops/services.json chat-4b max_model_len and fails on unknown services.
Tests chunk_budget_chars arithmetic and loud failures for insufficient windows.
Tests deterministic chunk_text on paragraphs, long lines, and coverage of every non-whitespace character.
FIT path compares run_chunked output directly against run_role on the same small input.
OVER path exercises compose_mode=rule deterministic concat and compose_mode=role reduce_synth.
F3 law test poisons the first chunk and expects a RuntimeError with a legible message instead of composing survivors.
Checks that run_chunked emits only run_items and run_reduce per-fan events, with no extra resolve/approve/dispatch verbs.
Monkeypatches cognition.client.complete for mock and failure scenarios.
standouts: Explicit F3 law: any failed or skipped chunk aborts composition rather than silently truncating the document.
Live-or-mocked acceptance design with a schema-valid stub for FocusOut and ReduceSynthOut shapes.
Coverage law assertion that chunked text preserves every non-whitespace character.
conventions: Uses a module-level global PASS counter and a check helper that prints ok/fail lines.
Directly monkeypatches fabric client complete functions and restores them manually in finally blocks.
Creates a fresh FsStore under tempfile for each scenario.
Imports with noqa E402 after sys.path manipulation.
Acceptance-test style with printed progress labels rather than a standard unittest/pytest class.
concerns: Global mutable state and direct monkeypatching of cognition.client.complete without a context manager or automatic restoration.
Test outcome depends on an external live model and the contents of ops/services.json.
The live probe catches a bare Exception, which could mask unrelated setup errors.
The _mock and _poison_first helpers accept extra keyword arguments but the symbol list flattens **kw into a positional kw parameter.
notes: An acceptance test that treats over-context chunking as a first-class tier with loud failure semantics and additive integration into existing cognition primitives.
questions: What is the exact full contract of ChunkedResult beyond the fields asserted here?
How does run_reduce select the reduce role and schema for compose_mode=role?
Can chunk boundaries be made semantic rather than purely character-based?
How are token counts measured versus the approximate chars_per_token heuristic?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to prove the F3 over-context tier; the file does exactly that through registry window checks, fit/over paths, compose modes, and loud-failure tests.
apparent_themes: Registry-driven configuration with no hardcoded constants
intention_signals: The author wants chunk-and-compose to be a safe, observable, and behavior-preserving extension: it should only activate when needed, reuse existing map/reduce machinery, and treat any partial map failure as a total failure rather than producing a truncated document.
novelty: Explicit F3 law that a failed chunk must abort composition rather than silently truncate, and a coverage law that chunk joins must preserve every non-whitespace character.
contribution: A concrete acceptance specification and test harness for the over-context tier, including the fit/over split, deterministic chunking invariants, two compose modes, and the loud-failure-on-partial-map rule.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for run_chunked that proves registry-based context-window chunking, fit-path byte-identity with run_role, over-context map-reduce composition, and loud failure when any chunk fails.
intention_for_embedding: To validate that oversized units are split, mapped, and composed safely without silent truncation and without altering existing role/items/reduce/swarm behavior.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints ok or fail.
symbols.description: A simple test harness function used throughout the file to assert conditions with a labeled message; it updates the module-level PASS counter and emits a per-check status line.

symbols.does: Probes the resident 4B model to decide whether to run live or mocked.
symbols.description: Attempts a minimal completion against cognition.RESIDENT_BASE_URL and RESIDENT_MODEL; any exception causes the test suite to fall back to mocked transport behavior.

symbols.does: Schema-valid stub object that exposes a model_dump method.
symbols.description: A tiny stand-in for a Pydantic-like response object, used by mocks to return dict-shaped role outputs that can be dumped via model_dump().

symbols.does: Stores the supplied dict on the instance.
symbols.description: Constructor for the _Validated stub; it simply assigns the input dictionary so later model_dump can return a copy.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the stored dict.
symbols.description: Imitates Pydantic's model_dump, returning the stored dictionary so mock role outputs look like normal structured completions.

symbols.does: Installs a mock fabric.client.complete that records user content and returns FocusOut or ReduceSynthOut shapes.
symbols.description: Replaces cognition.client.complete with a function that captures the last user message, distinguishes reduce_synth from focus by schema name, and returns deterministic stub outputs; returns the original complete function for restoration.

symbols.does: The inner mock completion that records prompts and returns stub outputs.
symbols.description: Appends the last user content to a shared list, inspects the requested schema name to decide between a reduce summary and a focus intent, and returns a _Validated dict; it accepts extra keyword arguments for compatibility with the real client signature.

symbols.does: Deterministic reduce rule that joins chunk intent fields into one dict.
symbols.description: A pure function passed as reduce_rule for compose_mode=rule; it collects the intent value from each mapped chunk output and returns a dict containing the merged list and count.

symbols.does: Mock completion that raises on the first call and succeeds thereafter.
symbols.description: Used to simulate a single chunk failure during the F3 law test; the first invocation raises a FabricError, while later invocations return a stub focus output, forcing run_chunked to abort composition.
